steps.title: Find the central feeling
steps.content: The poem centers on two companions whose "deeper content was never spoken" but who still understood each other. The peaceful natural setting supports that sense of quiet harmony and wordless understanding.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice A overstates the role of scenery; the poem does not list many sights. Choice B invents repulsion toward nature. Choice C treats the title as literal literary translation, which the poem does not discuss. The main purpose is the companions' unspoken bond. The answer is D.
